column1: A context graph system with an LLM-powered natural language query interface built over an SAP Order-to-Cash dataset.
│  D3.js force-directed graph + React chat interface  │
- LLMs understand SQL natively — simpler than Cypher/GQL
- Ideal for the dataset size (hundreds to thousands of records)
- Server computes graph topology from relational data (NetworkX-style)
- D3 force simulation renders it in-browser with drag
- No separate graph database needed — the graph is a **view** of the relational data
- Schema-injected system prompt enables accurate SQL generation
- Low temperature (0.05) for deterministic query output
- Falls back to rule-based SQL if no API key provided

column2: pre-LLM): rejects known off-topic patterns instantly

column1: 2. **System prompt instruction** (LLM-side): model told to return `GUARDRAIL:` prefix for off-topic queries
- Both layers return: *"This system is designed to answer questions related to the provided dataset only."*
- Full schema with column names and value domains
- Explicit instructions to return only JSON (no markdown)
- `highlighted_nodes` field — maps query results back to graph nodes for visual correlation
